channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A driver rams an anti-government rally in Serbia's capital and injures one protester
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A woman rammed a car into a crowd of anti-government protesters in Serbia's capital and injured one of them Friday, police said, as a student-led strike shut down businesses and drew tens of thousands of people to demonstrations around the country.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the escalating conflict in eastern Congo as rebels close in on Goma
item.description: Fighting has escalated sharply in recent weeks in eastern Congo, where rebels have seized key towns and are closing in on the city of Goma, the government’s last stronghold in the North Kivu province, where Goma is the provincial capital and which borders Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: Monika Pronczuk And Mark Banchereau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump had ‘fiery’ call with Danish prime minister over Greenland: Report
item.description: President Trump reportedly held a “fiery” call with Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen over the president’s insistence that U.S. control of Greenland is necessary for American national security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases names of 4 female hostages to be released next, possibly violates deal
item.description: Hamas says it will release four female IDF soldiers including Karina Ariev, Daniella Gilboa, Naama Levy and Liri Albag in second hostage release in a possible violation of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu suggests Israel might not complete its withdrawal from Lebanon by a ceasefire deadline
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu suggested Friday that Israel might not withdraw all of its forces from Lebanon by a deadline set in its ceasefire with Hezbollah, and Washington appears prepared to push for an extension.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tens of thousands protest in Slovakia over pro-Russia policies of populist leader Fico
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people in Slovakia gathered at squares and street across the country on Friday to voice their opposition to the pro-Russia policies of populist Prime Minister Robert Fico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Governor of Congo's North Kivu province dies of his wounds in fighting with rebels, authorities say
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The governor of eastern Congo’s North Kivu province has died from injuries sustained during fighting on the front line, a military spokesman said Friday, as M23 rebels closed in on the provincial capital.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New pandas make their debut at National Zoo
item.description: The National Zoo announced pandas have returned to Washington and made their public debut Friday. “The wait is OVER: the giant pandas have officially made their public debut at the Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: China's Lunar New Year travel rush kicks into high gear with 9 billion trips expected
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China’s Lunar New Year travel rush has kicked into high gear, with billions of trips expected in coming days for the peak of the 40-day annual mass migration — the world's biggest annual movement of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin echoes Trump's claim that conflict in Ukraine could have been avoided had he been in office
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has echoed U.S. President Donald Trump’s claim the conflict in Ukraine could have been prevented had he been in the White House in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Desperate families await return of 4 female soldiers held by Hamas for 15 months
item.description: Hamas has announced it would release four female soldiers held hostage for 15 months in Gaza, as part of an exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners laid out in its ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Shlomo Mor And Waafa Shurafa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taliban reject court move to arrest top officials for persecuting Afghan women and girls
item.description: The Taliban Friday rejected a court move to arrest two of their top officials for persecuting women, accusing the court of baseless accusations and misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major storm lashes Ireland and Scotland as hurricane-force winds down power lines and ground flights
item.description: Millions of people in Ireland and northern parts of the U.K. have heeded the advice of authorities to stay at home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal will unfold
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israeli troops have pulled back to the edges of Gaza, the first hostages have been released and many Palestinians have returned to what remains of their homes in the first few days of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, SAMY MAGDY and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio set to travel to Panama on first trip
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is traveling to Panama late next week in his first official diplomatic trip late, amid heightened tensions over President Trump’s threats to retake the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Haitians demand priority after government spends $3.8M for a brief visit from Colombia's leader
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The money materialized as soon as Colombia’s president confirmed his visit to southern Haiti.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Davos 2025: Some notable quotes from the World Economic Forum meeting
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Leading business and political figures attending the World Economic Forum meeting in Davos, Switzerland, have discussed and debated topics such as technology, tariffs, climate change, Ukraine, Gaza and the global economy this week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Croatia warns against travel to Serbia after 5 civil society activists were deported
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia warned its citizens Thursday to postpone unnecessary travel to neighboring Serbia after the deportation of five Croatian activists who had taken part in a meeting of civil society organizations in the Serbian capital, Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump talks tough with Putin on ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump is turning up the heat on Russian President Vladimir Putin in his first days in power, seeking to pressure Russia’s leader to come to the negotiating table for a deal to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: 2 people are killed in a knife attack in Germany; Scholz says there must be consequences
item.description: A stabbing attack in Germany has left two people dead, including a 2-year-old boy, and three others injured; the suspect was arrested shortly after the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama protests 'threat' of force as Trump pushed to reclaim canal
item.description: The Panamanian government formally raised concerns with the United Nations over President Trump’s threats to retake the Panama Canal, noting any threat of force would violate rules. In a letter to U.N. Secretary General António Guterres, Panama points to U.N.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan Air Force officer killed after being ‘inhaled’ by fighter jet’s engine
item.description: A Taiwan Air Force officer died after being sucked into the engine of an Indigenous Defense Fighter jet at Ching Chuan Kang Air Base, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of missing Marine veteran calls Trump admin a 'breath of fresh air' as she continues 12-year search
item.description: Debra Tice, mother of missing Marine veteran and American journalist Austin Tice, has returned to Syria to search for her son who was taken captive in August 2012.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military launches large-scale counterterrorism operation in West Bank city
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces announced an operation in the West Bank city of Jenin, which is a stronghold for terror organizations backed by Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
